title: Committing and pushing code changes

turns.content:
I’m checking the repository state and the workspace instructions first, then I’ll stage, commit, and push only if the tree is in a sensible state for that.

I’m loading the workspace operating instructions and memory files now so the git step follows the repo’s rules instead of guessing.

I’ve loaded the workspace rules and long-term context. Next I’m checking the actual git root, what is modified, and whether anything is already staged so the commit is deliberate rather than blanket.
